eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Caroline Elliott as the frontrunner in the British Columbia Conservative Party leadership election due to a Pallas Data poll released four days ago showing her at 31% first-choice support among party members—well ahead of Kerry-Lynne Findlay (24%) and Iain Black (18%)—coupled with high ballot exhaustion in the preferential voting system that advantages the top contender. High-profile endorsements from former figures like John Day and others in Kelowna two days ago have further solidified her momentum, while Peter Milobar's standing has slipped amid a campaign manager investigation in April. Findlay holds as a strong second with federal MP experience appealing to undecided voters, as membership swells past 42,000 ahead of the May vote. Black maintains third on former cabinet minister credentials, with Yuri Fulmer trailing amid early clashes with Elliott.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Toronto be 8°C or below on May 8?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Toronto Pearson Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 8 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Toronto Pearson Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/ca/mississauga/CYYZ.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Environment Canada's forecast, issued late May 7, projects Toronto's high temperature at Pearson International Airport on May 8 at 15°C under a mix of sun and cloud with 30% chance of showers, anchoring trader consensus around 14–15°C outcomes at 71.5% combined implied probability. Cool morning conditions near 5°C and persistent cloud cover are capping solar heating, positioning 14°C as the leading outcome at 42% amid a stable cooler-than-normal air mass—2–3°C below the early-May climatological average of 16°C—following recent swings like 17°C on May 5 and 12°C on May 7. Model consensus supports limited warming potential, with afternoon hourly observations key to resolution via Wunderground data; uncertainty persists from variable cloudiness and possible showers.

markets.question: Will Coinbase Global, Inc. (COIN) hit (LOW) $190 Week of May 4 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during the week of May 4 2026, any 1-minute candle for Coinbase Global, Inc. (COIN) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Coinbase Global, Inc. (COIN) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.COIN%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.COIN%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will Coinbase Global, Inc. (COIN) hit Week of May 4 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Coinbase Global (COIN) share price traded around $200 early in the week of May 4, 2026, buoyed by Bitcoin surpassing $80,000 and legislative tailwinds from the Clarity Act, but tumbled to approximately $193 by May 8 following a disappointing Q1 earnings release on May 7. The company posted $1.4 billion in revenue—a 26% year-over-year decline missing estimates—coupled with a $394 million net loss versus expected profits, driven by reduced trading fees amid crypto market volatility despite record volumes and market share gains. Concurrent May 5 layoffs of 14% of staff (about 700 jobs) signal cost discipline for 2026 adjusted expenses of $4.3–$4.6 billion and an AI pivot, tempering trader sentiment ahead of potential Federal Reserve signals on risk assets.

markets.question: McNally vs. Swiatek: Match O/U 23.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Caty McNally and Iga Swiatek in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia, originally scheduled for May 8, 2026 at 5:00AM ET.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed across all sets equals or exceeds 24. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
All tiebreaks—including any Champions/Super tiebreak—count as one (1) game toward the total.
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official WTA statistics.
markets.groupItemTitle: Internazionali BNL d'Italia: Caty McNally vs Iga Swiatek Match O/U 23.5

events.title: Internazionali BNL d'Italia: Caty McNally vs Iga Swiatek
events.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Caty McNally and Iga Swiatek in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia, originally scheduled for May 8, 2026 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Caty McNally' if Caty McNally advances against Iga Swiatek.

This market will resolve to 'Iga Swiatek' if Iga Swiatek advances against Caty McNally.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Iga Swiatek, the fourth seed and clay-court specialist, holds a commanding 2-0 head-to-head edge over Caty McNally entering their Internazionali BNL d'Italia second-round clash on Rome's clay, despite Swiatek's recent admissions of subpar clay form last year and pre-tournament scrutiny ahead of Roland Garros. McNally advanced with a decisive 6-2, 6-3 upset over 18th seed Daria Kasatkina in the opener yesterday, boosting her ranking to No. 61 and signaling momentum from recent hard-court wins. Swiatek, with a first-round bye, benefits from superior rankings (No. 3), surface history, and stylistic matchup advantages in baseline rallies, though McNally's aggressive play and past set taken at 2025 Wimbledon add upset intrigue on outdoor clay. No injury concerns reported for either.

markets.question: Will Russia enter Vasylivka by May 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures any territory of Vasylivka, Donetsk Oblast, (48.357760° N, 37.038017° E) between market creation and the specified date (ET).

Territory will be considered captured if any part of the specified territory is shaded under a below specified layer on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either "Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine", “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. 

Once a qualifying condition is met, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

If Russia comes into control of the specified territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. However, an announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ukrainian forces have recently intensified counteractions on the Vasylivka front in Zaporizhzhia Oblast, infiltrating southern Prymorske and rolling back Russian gains from earlier in 2026 as of early May. Intense fighting persists near Plavni dachas, Stepnohirsk supply lines, and eastern Russian salients, stalling Moscow's positional advances amid heavy attrition. No verified Russian entry into Vasylivka has occurred in the past 30 days, with Kyiv securing key defensive positions. Traders monitor local reinforcements, drone strikes, and artillery duels that could tip momentum, alongside broader Donetsk escalations diverting Russian resources before the market's end-of-May resolution window.

markets.question: Will SpaceX's market cap be between $1.8T and $2.0T at market close on IPO day?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on SpaceX's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no SpaceX IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO before 2028".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on SpaceX’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders heavily favor a SpaceX IPO closing market cap exceeding $2 trillion (60.5% implied probability), driven by the company's confidential S-1 filing in early April 2026 and subsequent reports boosting the target valuation from $1.75 trillion to over $2 trillion for a potential mid-June Nasdaq debut. Accelerating employee share vesting and surging inflows into space ETFs reflect optimism around Starlink's satellite internet dominance and Starship's recent Version 3 milestones, including a full 33-engine static fire on Super Heavy Booster 19 ahead of Flight 12. Lower brackets trail due to this aggressive pricing, while "No IPO before 2028" sits at 2.8% amid roadshow expectations the week of June 15; regulatory scrutiny and Musk's voting control pose minor risks to the timeline.

markets.question: Will Denman Rooke win the 2026 Galway West by-election?
markets.description:
A by-election for a seat from the Galway West constituency in the Dáil Éireann, the lower house of the Irish parliament, is expected to take place sometime in 2026 to fill the vacancy left by the election of Catherine Connoly as President of Ireland.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the by-election for this Galway West seat in the Irish Dáil Éireann.

If voting does not take place in this election or the election results are not definitively known by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve solely based on the official election results published by the Irish government, specifically the Irish parliament or “Oireachtas” (https://www.oireachtas.ie/en/elections/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket favors Independent Ireland's Noel Thomas at 54% implied probability to win the Galway West by-election on May 22, reflecting his strong local profile as a Connemara councillor and appeal amid voter frustration with government handling of housing shortages, traffic gridlock, and the stalled Ring Road project. Fine Gael's Seán Kyne trails at 33.5%, buoyed by a narrow first-preference lead (17%-16%) in the fresh Irish Times/TG4/Ipsos poll released May 7, though traders appear skeptical given historical mid-term by-election trends where coalition parties have not won since 2014. Labour's Helen Ogbu at 14.4% rounds out the top tier on 12% first preferences, with her transfers potentially pivotal in this 17-candidate PR-STV contest amid fragmented support for Fianna Fáil, Sinn Féin, and others.

markets.question: Will Anže Logar be the next Prime Minister of Slovenia?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Slovenia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve to the next individual who is officially elected by the National Assembly (Državni zbor) and sworn in as Prime Minister of Slovenia following the next parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally sworn in. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is sworn in by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Slovenia; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following Slovenia's hung parliament after the March 22, 2026, elections—where Robert Golob's Freedom Movement secured 29 seats to Janez Janša's Slovenian Democratic Party's 28—Golob conceded coalition failure on April 20, shifting momentum to the center-right bloc. President Nataša Pirc Musar declined to nominate a prime minister-designate on April 25, prompting parliament's 14-day nomination window starting May 5. The National Assembly's April 29 approval of ministry reductions from 19 to 14, backed by SDS, New Slovenia, Democrats, and Resni.ca, advanced Janša's bid; on May 5, he confirmed coalition guidelines agreed, with details finalizing. Trader consensus at 92% reflects this bloc's 43 seats plus external support nearing the 46-vote threshold for confirmation. Challenges include negotiation breakdowns or insufficient votes triggering snap elections.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket favors self-funding businessman Perry Johnson at 55.5% to win Michigan's Republican gubernatorial primary on August 4, driven by his April 21 ballot access confirmation—erasing 2022 disqualification fears—and pledged $9 million spending edge to sway 18% undecideds in low-turnout contests, despite Glengariff polls (April 21-24, released May 5) showing U.S. Rep. John James leading 37%-20%. Escalating legal tensions, including Johnson's April 22 lawsuit alleging James' logo implies incumbency and dueling signature challenges filed May 7 alleging fraud by pro-rival PACs and activists, add uncertainty ahead of the Board of State Canvassers' May 28 certification vote. James' recent straw poll losses further pressure his 38.5% odds, while fragmented field leaves Evan Space and others trailing.

eventMetadata.context_description: With California's June 2 top-two primary just weeks away—ballots mailed May 4—traders price former Attorney General and HHS Secretary Xavier Becerra as the clear frontrunner to win the governorship at 48% implied probability, buoyed by his post-April polling surge from single digits to 23-24% after Rep. Eric Swalwell's abrupt withdrawal amid sexual misconduct allegations, consolidating Democratic support in the fragmented field. Billionaire Tom Steyer trails at 36%, bolstered by self-funding and consistent 14-15% poll shares amid recent debates highlighting affordability and wealth tax divides. Republicans Steve Hilton (6.5%) and Chad Bianco lead some surveys but face steep general election hurdles in deep-blue California, where top-two dynamics risk pitting two Democrats against each other. High undecideds (23%) and volatility keep the race fluid ahead of early voting.

markets.question: Will NYC Mayor post 120-139 posts from May 5 to May 12, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani (@NYCMayor), posts on X between May 5, 12:00 PM ET and May 12, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the "Close" price for the BTC/USDT 1 hour candle that ends on the time and date specified in the title is higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1h" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Beijing be 30°C on May 8?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Beijing Capital International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 8 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Beijing Capital International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/beijing/ZBAA.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Observational data from Beijing Capital International Airport's official weather station (ZBAA), as reported via Wunderground and aligned with China Meteorological Administration guidance, confirm the day's peak temperature at 28°C, driving trader consensus to a near-certain 100% implied probability. This reflects real-time measurements capturing typical spring diurnal heating of 8–14°C from morning lows around 14°C, capped by urban aerosol haze and moderating southerly winds, consistent with ECMWF and GFS model runs projecting no further intensification amid declining solar angles post-3 PM. Above early-May climatological averages of 24–27°C—following recent warmer days like 30°C on May 5—challenges would require an improbable late-afternoon heat burst or rare data revision upon finalization, expected soon after local midnight.

markets.question: Will there be no Somaliland parliamentary election before 2027?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are to be scheduled to be held in Somaliland in late March 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Somaliland House of Representatives (Golaha Wakiilada) election. 

If no election takes place by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No election before 2027".

If an election takes place but the results are not known definitively by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will remain open until the certified results are released. If the results are not known definitively by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Somaliland Parliament. 

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Somaliland government, specifically the Somaliland National Electoral Commission (Komishanka Doorashooyinka Qaranka) (slnec.net).

eventMetadata.context_description: Somaliland's House of Elders approved a 27-month term extension for the House of Representatives on April 28, pushing parliamentary elections well beyond 2027 and driving trader consensus to 64.5% on no polls before then, amid repeated delays from an original May 2026 schedule. The National Electoral Commission had recommended a shorter extension for voter preparations, but the Guurti overrode it, sparking protests from opposition candidates decrying unconstitutional overreach. Waddani tops party odds at 32.6% on momentum from President Abdirahman Irro's 2024 presidential win under their banner, while former ruling Kulmiye trails at 0.3% post-defeat and UCID holds 4.0% as a perennial minor contender. Legal challenges and political pressures could still influence timelines.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket implies a highest temperature near 22°C for Ankara on May 10, with tightly clustered odds for 21–23°C reflecting short-range forecast uncertainty from the Turkish State Meteorological Service (MGM) and global models like ECMWF and GFS. After a sharp cold snap earlier this month with highs dipping to 8–10°C amid an Arctic airmass, southerly flows are ushering in seasonal recovery, but considerable cloudiness and 40–60% shower risks—per AccuWeather and Weather Channel guidance—cap daytime heating around the low-to-mid 20s Celsius. Model ensembles show divergence on afternoon cloud breaks and isolated thunderstorms, differentiating outcomes; watch MGM updates and new GFS runs by May 9 for refined guidance ahead of resolution. Historical May highs average 23–24°C, providing climatological context for this rebound.

markets.question: Will Trump say "Darth Vader" in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Donald Trump mentions the listed term between May 1, 2026, 12:00 AM ET and May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

A ‘mention’ will include any verbal mention which is recorded (audio or video) and publicly accessible.

Written usages of terms (e.g. Truth Social posts, written statements, etc.) will not count toward this market's resolution.

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

Videos posted on his social media which were filmed outside this market's time frame will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's repeated verbal warnings against Iranian nuclear ambitions during Strait of Hormuz ceasefire talks, including May 5 criticism of Pope Leo's stance, have driven trader consensus to price "nuke" at 79% probability on Polymarket's multi-outcome market tracking phrases he will verbally mention in recorded audio or video through May 31, 2026. Recent public appearances—such as May 1 remarks to seniors in Florida, May 4 White House small business summit address, and May 6 statements—have heightened activity without resolving other leading options like "Epstein" (66%) or "cat" (62%), underscoring his unscripted rhetorical patterns. Upcoming policy briefings, White House events, and potential foreign policy summits could prompt shifts, with resolution strictly limited to verbal mentions excluding written posts.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NV-01 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NV-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Rep. Dina Titus's commanding fundraising lead—$1.5 million raised and over $1 million cash-on-hand—over Democratic primary challengers Joy Hoover, Gabriel Cornejo, and Luis Paniagua bolsters trader consensus for a Democratic hold at 84% in Nevada's 1st Congressional District, a D+2 partisan lean area. Recent April 29 reporting highlights intra-party criticism of Titus's Laken Riley Act vote amid calls for generational change, but her historical general election margins (52% in 2024) and weak challenger resources minimize primary risks ahead of June 9. A fragmented Republican primary field, including state Sen. Carrie Buck and others, limits GOP viability per Likely Democratic ratings from Cook and Sabato's Crystal Ball.

markets.question: Taiwanese Premier Cho Jung-tai out by June 30, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Cho Jung-tai ceases to be Premier of Taiwan for any period of time between market creation and the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Cho Jung-tai's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

If the specified individual is detained, effectively removed from the specified position, or otherwise permanently prevented from fulfilling the duties of the specified position within this market’s timeframe, it will qualify for a “Yes” resolution.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Cho Jung-tai and the government of Taiwan; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Taiwan's Premier Cho Jung-tai, leading the Democratic Progressive Party (DPP) minority government under President Lai Ching-te, remains in office amid chronic gridlock with the opposition-controlled Legislative Yuan, where Kuomintang (KMT) and Taiwan People's Party (TPP) hold a majority. Tensions peaked in late 2025 and early 2026 with Cho's refusals to countersign opposition-passed amendments on fiscal revenue, spending, and legal revisions—citing constitutional violations—prompting censure motions that advanced but failed to trigger a no-confidence vote. No major developments have emerged in the past 30 days; Cho recently directed aid for stranded shipping firms and delivered policy reports. Upcoming central budget reviews and legislative sessions could reignite clashes, while 2026 local elections loom as a potential catalyst for cabinet reshuffles.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 69.5% implied probability for Guangzhou's highest temperature reaching 25°C or higher on May 9, driven primarily by the latest China Meteorological Administration forecast projecting a daytime high of exactly 25°C amid thundershowers and cloudy skies. This reflects suppressed solar heating from persistent cloud cover and precipitation over the Pearl River Delta, following a similar pattern that capped May 8's high near 28°C and earlier days around 25-26°C despite May climatological averages of 29°C. High humidity in the subtropical monsoon regime amplifies cooling effects from showers, though model ensembles like CMA and global runs indicate uncertainty around exact peak, with potential for 24°C if showers intensify. Traders await final observations from Guangzhou Baiyun International Airport station, with updated forecasts expected overnight.

markets.question: Kawkab AC vs. RCA Zemamra: O/U 3.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Morocco Botola Pro game between Kawkab AC and RCA Zemamra, scheduled for May 8 at 4:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Kawkab AC and RCA Zemamra combine to score 4 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 4, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on frmf.ma. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Kawkab AC vs. RCA Zemamra - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Morocco Botola Pro game, scheduled for May 8 at 4:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kawkab Marrakech hosts RCA Zemamra in Botola Pro Round 19's return leg on May 8 at Grand Stadium of Marrakech, following their 1-1 first-leg draw in October 2025. Both mid-table sides vie for points in a congested standings battle, with Kawkab around 9th-12th on roughly 15-16 points from 16 games marked by defensive solidity (eight draws) but scant goals, while Zemamra sits nearby after a 1-2 home loss to MAS Fes on April 26 exposed defensive frailties amid mixed recent form. Kawkab's home advantage and head-to-head parity could tilt trader consensus, though no major injuries or suspensions alter lineups; watch pre-match team news for starting XI shifts in this evenly matched fixture.

markets.question: Metamask FDV above $500M one day after launch?
markets.description:

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Metamask doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Will Mike Kennealy win the 2026 Massachusetts Governor Republican primary election?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for Governor of Massachusetts, scheduled to take place on September 1, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Massachusetts Gubernatorial Republican Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Massachusetts Republican Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: Massachusetts Governor Republican Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for Governor of Massachusetts, scheduled to take place on September 1, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Massachusetts Gubernatorial Republican Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Massachusetts Republican Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mike Minogue's landslide victory at the Massachusetts Republican Party convention on April 26, capturing over 70% of delegate votes for the gubernatorial endorsement, has driven trader consensus to price him at 80.5% to win the September 1 Republican primary. This momentum, combined with recent polls showing a 13-point lead, reflects his appeal as a political newcomer emphasizing fiscal accountability amid voter frustration with rising costs. Brian Shortsleeve qualified for the ballot with 15% support but has seen odds drop to 11.8% amid Minogue's pressure campaign for party unity and refusal to debate. Mike Kennealy's 1.4% reflects failure to clear the convention threshold, narrowing the field despite his prior polling strength. The crowded primary has consolidated behind Minogue as the strongest general election challenger to incumbent Gov. Maura Healey.

markets.question: Will Jorge Rodríguez be the leader of Venezuela end of 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the individual who officially holds the position of the head of state of Venezuela on Dec 31, 2026 at 12 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, "officially holds" refers to the individual that was formally appointed, confirmed (if confirmation is required), and sworn in as the head of state of Venezuela or otherwise confirmed by official government information as being the head of state of Venezuela. 

If the Venezuelan government does not clearly state who is the head of state, the market will resolve to the individual who is listed as the Head of State by the UN (see: https://www.un.org/dgacm/en/content/protocol/hshgnfa).  

In the event that more than one official head of state is listed by the Venezuelan  government, this market will resolve to the individual who is listed as having primary status. If no distinction is made, the market will resolve to the individual who first assumed the position. 

If no individual holds the position this market will resolve to “No Head of State”. 

The following do NOT constitute "officially holding" the role: nominated, announced, or designated as the head of state of Venezuela but appointment not yet effective; appointed with an effective date after Dec 31, 2026; previously served as the head of state of Venezuela but term has expired, resigned, or been terminated before Dec 31, 2026; serving as the head of state of Venezuela in a consultant, contractor, or unofficial capacity; named as successor or heir apparent to the head of state of Venezuela but not yet appointed; or holding a different role with oversight of the head of state of Venezuela but not the specified position itself.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the UN recognized government of Venezuela. If the Government of Venezuela does not clearly state who is the head of state, information from the UN and a consensus of credible reporting may be used. 

Note: this market is mutually exclusive.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Nicolás Maduro as the frontrunner at 62% to lead Venezuela by December 31, 2026, despite his January 3 U.S. military capture and ongoing New York trial where he recently won approval for government-funded legal defense, fueling bets on potential release, extradition failure, or sustained UN recognition of his administration. Delcy Rodríguez, Maduro's former vice president sworn as acting president by the Supreme Tribunal of Justice on January 5, follows at 23% after replacing senior military commanders in March and declining to announce elections, signaling continuity amid fragile U.S.-Venezuela commercial thaw. Opposition leader María Corina Machado lags at 8%, with no transitional government or snap election in sight as chavismo structures hold.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Houston be between 88-89°F on May 9?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the William P. Hobby Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 9 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the William P. Hobby Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/tx/houston/KHOU.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket tilts modestly toward a Houston high of 80-81°F (27% implied probability) on May 9, with 82-83°F (21%) and 78-79°F (16.5%) close behind, capturing model ensemble spread amid inherent 2-3 day forecast uncertainty. National Weather Service guidance and major models (GFS, ECMWF, Canadian) converge on afternoon highs near 81°F under partly cloudy conditions, moderated by Gulf of Mexico moisture fueling sea breeze circulation and potential cumulus cloud development that caps peak heating. Differentiating factors include divergent simulations of diurnal convection timing, boundary layer winds (northerly shear vs. southerly flow), and residual soil moisture from prior showers limiting sensible heating below climatological May norms of ~86°F. Watch 00Z model runs overnight and NWS updates tomorrow for refinements ahead of resolution.

markets.question: LoL: JD Gaming vs Bilibili Gaming - Game 1 Winner
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between JD Gaming and Bilibili Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for May 8 at 7:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "JD Gaming" if JD Gaming win Game 1 against Bilibili Gaming.

This market will resolve to "Bilibili Gaming" if Bilibili Gaming win Game 1 against JD Gaming.

If the match begins but is not completed, and Game 1 is concluded with a winner determined, this market will resolve based on the completed Game 1. 

If Game 1 is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. 

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without play beginning, this market will resolve 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: LoL: JD Gaming vs Bilibili Gaming (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
events.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between JD Gaming and Bilibili Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for May 8 at 7:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "JD Gaming" if JD Gaming win the match against Bilibili Gaming.

This market will resolve to "Bilibili Gaming" if Bilibili Gaming win the match against JD Gaming.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Bilibili Gaming tops LPL Split 2 Group Ascend standings at 8-2 series record ahead of JD Gaming's 7-3, positioning both for direct playoff bracket entry in the double round-robin BO3 format with Fearless Draft, where top 4 advance outright. BLG, fresh off their Split 1 Grand Final triumph over JDG (3-1), claimed a 2-1 victory in their Week 1 head-to-head here, bolstering their +9 game differential on a three-win streak. JDG counters with a six-win streak and +6 differential, showcasing consistent macro execution and laning edges in recent sweeps. No roster changes or substitutions reported; outcome hinges on draft adaptation and skirmish control in this pivotal rematch for seeding dominance.

markets.question: Will fewer than 25 ships transit the Strait of Hormuz between April 27-May 3?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the total number of transit calls that IMF Portwatch reports for the Strait of Hormuz for all days from April 27, 2026, through May 3, 2026, inclusive.

Transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as data has been published for the final date in the specified period. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for the final date of the specified period, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

Daily passages averaged 5-8 vessels—five on April 24 per shipping reports and eight on April 27—amid Iranian restrictions, US port blockades, and attack threats that stranded over 1,000 vessels and disrupted 20% of global oil flows. This skin-in-the-game pricing reflects 95% drops from pre-war norms of 140 daily transits, though final IMF Portwatch tallies or undetected dark fleet activity could prompt minor revisions.

eventMetadata.context_description: Alexander Blockx's implied 67.5% probability reflects his explosive clay-court form during the spring swing, including a breakthrough semifinal run at the Mutua Madrid Open where the 21-year-old Belgian upset defending champion Casper Ruud and notched three straight Top 20 victories before falling to Alexander Zverev. Now ranked a career-high No. 36, Blockx backed his momentum with a gritty three-set comeback over wildcard Federico Cina in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia first round Thursday. Tallon Griekspoor, seeded No. 29 at No. 31 overall with an 8-10 record this year, enters via a bye after a modest Madrid showing—edging Damir Dzumhur before losing to Lorenzo Musetti—with no head-to-head history favoring the Dutchman's experience on the slower Roman clay.

eventMetadata.context_description: Blake Miguez holds a clear trader consensus edge at 52.5% implied probability in the May 16 Republican primary for Louisiana's open 5th Congressional District seat, driven by his dominant fundraising with $4 million cash on hand reported mid-April, President Trump's early endorsement, and the NRA's sole backing in the race announced April 28. A Rainey Center poll from early April showed a tight contest with Miguez at 23% and Michael Echols at 20% among likely voters, but Miguez's financial and America First credentials have widened his lead despite Echols' local residency advantage and attacks labeling Miguez a carpetbagger from outside the district. Recent independent expenditures include pro-Echols funding from GOPAC and Louisiana Growth And Future Fund in late April, countered by anti-Miguez ads, as early voting continues ahead of election day.

Sinner's 28-match Masters 1000 winning streak underscores his improved clay prowess, positioning him to complete a career Grand Slam amid a depleted field. Rising Spaniard Rafael Jódar (4.0%) and French hopefuls Arthur Fils (3.8%) and Lorenzo Musetti (2.1%) draw upset interest from recent breakthroughs and home-crowd support.

markets.question: Will Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) hit (LOW) $570 Week of May 4 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during the week of May 4 2026, any 1-minute candle for Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.META%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.META%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

eventMetadata.context_description: Meta Platforms' (META) shares hovered around $617 during the week of May 4, reflecting trader digestion of Q1 2026 earnings released April 29, where revenue surged 33% year-over-year to $56.3 billion—beating consensus by $900 million—and diluted EPS hit $10.44 against $6.67 expected, driven by ad revenue strength across Facebook and Instagram. The post-earnings dip stemmed from elevated 2026 capex guidance of $125-145 billion for AI infrastructure, raising margin pressure fears despite operating income of $22.9 billion (41% margin). Analyst consensus holds at Strong Buy with a $840 average price target, implying 36% upside from current levels. Upcoming Q2 earnings on July 29 loom as the next catalyst amid tech sector volatility.

markets.question: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects deep skepticism over federal enactment of comprehensive AI safety legislation before 2027, driven by congressional gridlock and the Trump administration's March 2026 National AI Policy Framework advocating light-touch rules that preempt burdensome state laws while prioritizing innovation over stringent safety mandates. Recent bipartisan advances, such as the Senate Judiciary Committee's unanimous April 30 approval of the GUARD Act mandating age verification and banning AI companions for minors, remain narrowly focused on child protection rather than broad risk classification or high-risk AI oversight. No major safety bill has cleared key committees amid competing appropriations battles and the 119th Congress's lame-duck session approaching, with states like Oregon enacting chatbot safeguards on May 1, underscoring the federal void.

No prior head-to-head exists; trader consensus favors Yellow Submarine's CIS experience and form in this double-elimination BO3, though REKONIX's upset potential in recent Tier 2 qualifiers adds competitiveness amid teamfight-heavy meta shifts.

markets.question: Will another result occur for the next governing coalition of New Zealand?
markets.description:
A general election is scheduled to be held in New Zealand on November 7, 2026.

This market will resolve to the first ruling coalition of parties that forms after the next New Zealand general election.

A party will only be considered part of the ruling coalition if it participates in the governing coalition and provides at least one Cabinet minister. Parties that merely support the government from outside Cabinet, including through confidence-and-supply agreements or similar arrangements, without holding a Cabinet post will not qualify.

The following parties will be considered for this market: ACT New Zealand (ACT), Labour Party (Labour), Te Pāti Māori (Maori), National Party (National), Green Party (Green), and New Zealand First Party (NZF). All other parties will not be considered.

Resolution will be based on the listed option that most completely matches the parties included in the coalition. If the coalition formed after the election includes all of the parties listed in a market option, along with any other parties, that option will resolve to “Yes” unless there exists another market option that more completely covers the coalition. A listed option will not resolve to “Yes” if any of its listed parties are not included in the coalition.

For example:

- If the governing coalition includes Labour + Green + ACT, but no option explicitly lists those three together, the market will resolve to “Labour + Green” if that option is listed and no listed option more completely matches the coalition.

- If the governing coalition includes National + ACT + NZF, the option “National + ACT + NZF” will resolve to “Yes”, while “National + ACT” will resolve to “No”.

In the event that the ruling coalition includes all parties for multiple market options, and each option contains an equal number of coalition parties, the tie will be resolved in favor of the option whose listed parties together hold the greater number of seats in the newly elected House of Representatives. If this presents another tie, this market will resolve in favor of the listed option whose parties received the greatest number of valid party votes.

If any party forms a single-party majority government, the listed option for that party will be considered the governing coalition.

If the governing coalition does not include all parties in any listed option, this market will resolve to “Other”.

This market may resolve once the first government is officially confirmed following the appointment of the Prime Minister and ministers by the Governor-General after the 2026 New Zealand general election, with ministers appointed under non-caretaker circumstances.

If the next New Zealand ruling coalition after the election is not confirmed by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

This market will resolve based on a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve based on official information from the government of New Zealand.

markets.question: Will London have less than 5mm of precipitation in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the total precipitation in mm at Heathrow (London Airport) in May, 2026, according to the Met Office.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The resolution source for this market will be the Met Office, specifically the figure for May 2026 under "rain mm" at the https://www.metoffice.gov.uk/pub/data/weather/uk/climate/stationdata/heathrowdata.txt link once the Provisional figure for the whole month of May 2026 is released.

If the relevant data is not available by July 7, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, another credible resolution source will be chosen.

The resolution source for this market measures precipitation to 1 decimal place (e.g., 1.5), thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving this market. Any revisions to precipitation recorded after Provisional data is released for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: David Njoku remains an unsigned free agent nearly two weeks after the 2026 NFL Draft, fueling trader consensus with top outcomes tightly clustered around 45-47% implied probability among the Bengals, Chargers, Cardinals, Broncos, and Dolphins. The 29-year-old tight end's February farewell to the Browns—coupled with Cleveland's veteran TE signing and draft additions signaling a roster rebuild—has dimmed re-sign odds to 24.5%, opening a competitive market. These frontrunners share cap space exceeding $20 million each, thin tight end rooms lacking seam-stretchers, and young QBs like Joe Burrow, Justin Herbert, Kyler Murray, Bo Nix, and Tua Tagovailoa needing reliable red-zone targets. Recent visits to the Chargers and Dolphins, plus Broncos' pre-draft TE urgency, underscore the fluid dynamics with no clear leader emerging.

markets.question: Will US unemployment reach at least 5.0% in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any seasonally adjusted unemployment rate (total unemployed, as a percent of the civilian labor force, official unemployment rate denoted as U-3) reported by the Bureau of Labor Statistics in an “Employment Situation Report” for a reference month in 2026 is greater than or equal to the listed percentage. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The relevant reports for this market are the Employment Situation Reports for January-December, 2026. This market may not resolve to “No” until the Employment Situation report for December 2026 is released. If no Employment Situation Report for December 2026 is released by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, however, this market will resolve based on all previously published data up to that time. 

The resolution source for this market is the Monthly Employment Situation Report, published by the BLS every month at https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/empsit.htm, specifically the U-3 measure in Table A-15 for each month.

Note: the resolution source for this market reports unemployment to one decimal point. Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders assign a 39% implied probability to US unemployment peaking at 5.0% in 2026, higher than Federal Reserve median projections of 4.4% for Q4 amid resilient but softening labor conditions. March's unemployment rate held steady at 4.3% with nonfarm payrolls adding 178,000 jobs, while initial jobless claims for the week ending May 2 rose modestly to 200,000—still near cycle lows on a four-week average of 203,250—signaling low layoffs despite economic headwinds. Forecasts from the Philly Fed and CBO point to a gradual rise toward 4.5-4.6%, tempered by steady inflation and Fed funds stability post-April FOMC. Key catalysts include today's April employment report, monthly BLS releases, and June FOMC policy signals on rate path amid slowing GDP growth.

markets.question: Will Analilia Mejia win the NJ-11 Special Election by 40% or more?
markets.description:
The special election for the seat for New Jersey's 11th congressional district in the U.S. House of Representatives is currently scheduled to take place on April 16, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two candidates in the specified election.

For the purpose of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid votes received by the first-place and second-place candidates. Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher placed bracket.

If two or more candidates tie for the most valid votes in this election, and the tie is between two candidates listed, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the candidate whose listed last name comes first in alphabetical order. If the tie is between a listed candidate and an unlisted candidate, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the listed candidate. If the tie is between two or more unlisted candidates, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the count has been made official.

If the results of the specified election are not known definitively by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including https://www.nj.gov/state/.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

eventMetadata.context_description: Democrat Analilia Mejia's commanding 60%-39% victory over Republican Joe Hathaway in New Jersey's 11th Congressional District special election on April 16 has solidified trader consensus on a 20-25% margin, confirmed by official final results certified around May 4. The D+5 district's safely blue status, low special election turnout of about 136,000 voters on a Thursday, and Mejia's outsized wins in urban and Hispanic-heavy areas like Dover—outperforming 2024 Democratic presidential margins by 25+ points—drove the landslide, despite underperformance in Jewish suburbs like Millburn and Livingston. With the Polymarket awaiting any recounts until June 30, slim odds on alternatives reflect minimal risk of shifts absent major vote count errors or legal challenges.

markets.question: Bank of England decreases interest rates by 50+ bps after July 2026 meeting?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the change in basis points in the Bank Rate resulting from the July 2026 meeting of the Bank of England’s Monetary Policy Committee, relative to the level it was prior to this meeting.

The resolution source will be official information from the Bank of England, including the statement or release from its July 2026 Monetary Policy Committee meeting, scheduled for July 30, 2026, as listed on the official Bank of England calendar (https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/monetary-policy/upcoming-mpc-dates). This market may resolve as soon as the statement or release of the Bank of England's July 2026 Monetary Policy Committee meeting with relevant data is issued.

If the specified rate is defined by an upper and lower bound, the relevant change will be the change to the upper bound.

If the specified rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded according to the following guidelines. Increases or decreases of less than 25 bps will be rounded to 25 bps (e.g. an increase or decrease of 10 bps would be considered to be an increase or decrease of 25 bps). Increases or decreases of greater than 25 bps will be rounded to the nearest 25 bps and will be rounded away from 0 in cases of equidistance (e.g., an increase or decrease of 37.5 bps would be considered to be an increase or decrease of 50 bps). Displayed options of “Increase” or “Decrease” will include policy rate increases or decreases of any size.

If the specified meeting is postponed to a date and time before the start of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve based on the outcome of that postponed meeting. If the specified meeting is cancelled, or postponed such that no decision is announced by the start of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the “No Change” bracket. Emergency changes to the specified rate not resulting from the specified meeting will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price evenly matched implied probabilities of 49.5% across all Bank of England July Monetary Policy Committee outcomes—from 25 basis points or 50+ bps hikes/cuts to no change—reflecting deep uncertainty over the rate path amid conflicting signals. March 2026 CPI accelerated to 3.3% year-over-year from 3.0%, fueled by energy and motor fuel spikes from Middle East conflict, leading to a unanimous MPC hold at 3.75% Bank Rate. Resilient labor conditions, with unemployment falling to 4.9% in February, counterbalance disinflation forces, while persistent services inflation sustains hike risks. Pivotal catalysts include today's April 30 decision, upcoming May/June CPI data, and energy price developments ahead of the July 30 meeting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Bigetron by Vitality sits third in MPL Indonesia Season 17 regular season standings at 6-4 (13-13 games), one match win ahead of fourth-place Team Liquid ID (6-5, 13-14), fueling trader focus on their Week 7 BO3 rematch scheduled for May 8. BTR's trader consensus edge stems from a 2-1 head-to-head win over TLID on April 18, extended by recent sweeps including 2-0 over RRQ Hoshi on May 2 and EVOS earlier in April, showcasing strong form amid patch 2.1.61 meta. TLID showed resilience with a 2-1 upset over NAVI on May 3 but faltered against top squads like ONIC and Dewa United. Both squads hold stable rosters—BTR featuring midlaner Moreno, TLID exp laner Aran—with top-6 playoff spots on the line in a congested race.

markets.question: Will Natural Gas (NG) hit (LOW) $2.00 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of Natural Gas futures has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For Natural Gas futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For Natural Gas (NG) futures, the last trading day is defined as the third last business day of the month prior to the contract month, consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month Natural Gas futures "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=NGD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published for the Active Month Natural Gas (NG) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will Natural Gas (NG) hit in May 2026?
events.description: What will Natural Gas (NG) hit in May 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Henry Hub natural gas spot prices hovered around $2.70–$2.80 per MMBtu in early May 2026, reflecting ample supply amid a smaller-than-expected 63 Bcf storage injection for the week ended May 1—below consensus forecasts of 74 Bcf—driven by declining LNG feedgas and moderate power sector demand. Total working gas inventories reached 2,205 Bcf, well above the five-year average, capping upside amid steady production near 104 Bcf/d and mild early-summer weather limiting air conditioning loads. The futures curve slopes upward, with June 2026 contracts at $2.78 and July nearing $3.00, signaling trader expectations for seasonal cooling demand. Key catalysts include Thursday's EIA storage report on May 15 and extended weather outlooks, which could sway sentiment if heat accelerates withdrawals.

markets.question: Israel x Hezbollah diplomatic meeting by May 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a diplomatic meeting between representatives of Israel and Hezbollah by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of Israel and Hezbollah who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding Israel-Hezbollah relations on behalf of their governments/leadership structures. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify. 

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not count.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect in-person meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the government of Israel and Hezbollah leadership, and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: U.S.-mediated direct talks between Israeli and Lebanese ambassadors, held in Washington since April 14, represent the first such diplomacy in decades, focusing on ceasefire implementation, Hezbollah disarmament north of the Litani River, and long-term security arrangements. Hezbollah, absent from these sessions and vocally opposing direct engagement with Israel, recently signaled openness to indirect negotiations via its secretary general on May 4. However, a fragile ceasefire from mid-April endures mutual violations, including Israel's May 7 airstrike killing a senior Hezbollah Radwan Force commander in Beirut amid ongoing rocket fire. A third round of Israel-Lebanon talks, including military representatives, is set for May 14-15, but Hezbollah's exclusion and persistent escalation underscore significant barriers to a qualifying in-person diplomatic meeting between Israel and Hezbollah by May 31.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Abdul El-Sayed as the frontrunner at 54.5% implied probability to win Michigan's Democratic US Senate primary on August 4, driven by his strong support among voters under 40—leading Mallory McMorrow 35%-18% in the recent Emerson poll—amid generational splits favoring progressives in early crosstabs. That April survey showed El-Sayed tied with McMorrow at 24% and ahead of Haley Stevens at 13%, with markets diverging from tied polling averages by betting on youth turnout and El-Sayed's momentum in this open-seat contest following Gary Peters' retirement. Sen. Debbie Stabenow's recent endorsement of Stevens provides establishment backing but has yet to shift trader sentiment significantly.

markets.question: Will the US acquire part of Greenland in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the United States acquires control of any land territory that is part of Greenland by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Only the transfer of sovereignty, or the acquisition of primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control qualifies.

1. Transfer of Sovereignty: This will qualify if a binding agreement or legal instrument results in a defined area of Greenland coming under the formal sovereignty of the U.S. (e.g., incorporated as a U.S. state, territory, possession, or other U.S. political classification), even if the effective date occurs after the market deadline.

2. Acquisition of Primary or Exclusive Jurisdiction or Control: This will qualify if a binding agreement or legal instrument establishes a defined area in Greenland in which the U.S. has primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control over the territory, such that the ordinary legal authority of Denmark and Greenland do not apply,except by U.S. permission. Such agreements or instruments will qualify even if the effective date occurs after the market deadline. 

3. Use of Force: If the U.S. acquires primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control over a defined area of Greenland through force, this will also qualify. 

An announcement will qualify only if it is accompanied by or consists of a binding agreement or legal instrument  (e.g., enacted legislation, a signed treaty, the signed text of an agreement, or an executive action implementing such an agreement) that unambiguously creates a transfer of sovereignty, or primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control,  even if this transfer or acquisition takes effect after the market deadline. 

Non-binding statements, negotiations, proposals, frameworks, or MOUs will not alone qualify. Basing rights, access agreements, SOFA-type arrangements, COFA-type arrangements, commercial concessions, or other permissions to use land (including leases) will not alone qualify. Any qualifying U.S. jurisdiction or control  in Greenland that existed at market creation will not count as new qualifying control.

Examples of qualifying events include but are not limited to treaty or piece of legislation that makes any portion of Greenland a U.S. territory or possession, even if the handover date for such territory or possession is later); or, a Guantánamo-style arrangement establishing a defined zone in Greenland under exclusive or primary U.S. jurisdiction and control, where Denmark and Greenland’s ordinary legal authority does not apply except by U.S. permission.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States, Denmark, and Greenland; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's second administration has pursued greater U.S. influence in Greenland citing Arctic national security needs, including a January 2026 announcement of a "framework for a future deal" and discussions of purchase offers up to $700 billion, but Denmark and Greenland's government have repeatedly rejected any sovereignty transfer, with 85% of Greenlanders opposing U.S. control amid widespread protests. Recent developments focus on U.S. negotiations for expanded military base access—such as three additional sites announced in April—and private American firms securing rare earth mining rights, rather than territorial acquisition. These diplomatic rebuffs from NATO ally Denmark, rooted in historical refusals and international law, underpin trader consensus pricing "No" at 86%, with no scheduled talks indicating a shift before year-end.

markets.question: Will New Slovenia – Christian Democrats (NSi) be part of the next Government of Slovenia?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Slovenia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed political party is included in the first Slovenian government formed after the 2026 Slovenian parliamentary elections. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may resolve once the first government is officially confirmed following a successful confirmation vote in the Slovenian National Assembly (Državni zbor), with ministers sworn in under non-caretaker circumstances.

A party will only be considered part of the coalition if it signs the coalition agreement and provides at least one cabinet minister. Parties that merely support the government (e.g., through confidence-and-supply arrangements, parliamentary support agreements, or similar) without signing the coalition agreement and holding a cabinet post will not qualify.

If no government is formed, or the results are not known definitively by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve based on a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve based on official information from the Government of Slovenia.
